title: Survey Sunday: what will it take for fleets to realize electric works – TODAY?
summary:
For the last few weeks, we’ve been running a sidebar survey about what Electrek readers think it would take to convince the most EV-resistant fleet operators to realize that electric vehicles are ready for work TODAY. After receiving 1,700 responses, here’s what you told us.

title: E-quipment highlight: 25-ton LiuGong 870HE electric wheel loader
summary:
Chinese equipment brand LiuGong is putting its 25-ton 870HE electric wheel loader to work at a STRABAG quarry in Slovenia, proving that electric vehicles can get the job done without the sky-high fuel costs associated with fossil fuels.

more…

title: Here are the best electric bikes you can buy at every price level in June 2026
summary:
I’ve spent countless hours here at Electrek doing detailed hands-on testing of hundreds of electric bikes. Through thousands of miles of riding, I’ve been fortunate to learn these e-bikes inside and out, top to bottom and front to back. That long-term experience with real-world e-bike testing has helped me find the best electric bicycles on the market for just about any budget.

Below are some of the top e-bikes I’ve hand-tested for every price range, current as of June 2026. With summer ready to hit us full force, it’s time to get back in the saddle! Check out the awesome e-bikes below, any one of which could become your next electric bike.

more…

title: New Danfoss hydraulic pump increases equipment runtime by 50%
summary:
Danish engineering and powertrain experts Danfoss have developed a new digital hydraulic system designed specifically for electric construction equipment that can reduce energy consumption from the machine’s battery by 35%, and improve runtime by more than 50%. (!)

more…

title: When public charging stations aren’t so public, and why it matters
summary:
Drivers opening an EV charging app for the first time today will be presented with thousands of so-called “public” chargers at local car dealers. Once they arrive, however, the store’s customers, store hours, security gates, and Wild West pricing can turn what should be a routine charging stop into a frustrating ordeal. For first-time EV drivers, that doesn’t just reflect poorly on the dealer – it reflects poorly on the EV, itself. And we need to fix it.

more…

title: Harbinger gears up for war with autonomous military truck program
summary:
Commercial truck startup Harbinger is partnering with the robotics experts at American Rheinmetall to develop an uncrewed military ground vehicle based on the company’s medium duty hybrid and electric truck chassis.

more…

title: All the new EVs you can buy with 0% financing in June 2026
summary:
Lease deals get all the hype, but a lot of people would still rather buy their vehicles. If that sounds like you, you’re in luck, because there’s a whole bunch of great EVs you can buy with 0% financing this month – including the best-selling Tesla Model Y!

title: Carvana’s bet on Slate is ACTUALLY a bet on itself, as the future of automotive retail
summary:
As new electric vehicle brands like Scout and Slate search for alternatives to franchise auto dealers, America’s largest online auto retailer is quietly positioning itself to become the future of automotive retail.

title: Wallbox deploys its first Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers
summary:
Wallbox just installed its first public Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers at Port de Sitges, a busy marina on Spain’s Mediterranean coast near Barcelona.

title: T1 Energy buys KORE Power to cash in on the AI power boom
summary:
T1 Energy, formerly known as FREYR Battery, is buying battery storage and software company KORE Power in a deal aimed at growing its presence in the fast-expanding energy storage and AI data center markets.

title: Tesla Model Y Battery Tested After 16,000 Miles Of Mostly Fast Charging
summary: This Model Y was predominantly fast-charged for six months but its battery remains very healthy.

title: Slate Could Have A Powerful New Friend In Carvana
summary: One online car dealer may have quietly secured its place in making Slate's dreams come true, according to a report.

title: Volkswagen Compares Gas Cars To Horses, Says That EVs Are Plain Better
summary: Horses were never banned, but people realized it’s easier to use gas cars to travel. VW says the same will happen with EVs.

title: Audi's Stunning New Nuvolari Supercar Is A Tri-Motor Plug-In Hybrid
summary: Audi’s most powerful car ever pairs three tiny axial flux motors with a screaming V-8 engine.

title: Tesla Wants Its Cameras To Blink Like a Human Eye
summary: Computers might be better at some things, but Tesla's newest patent shows that the human eye remains undefeated in one important area.

title: This 85% Scale Vintage Bentley Is One Of A Kind—And Electric
summary: The bespoke Bentley Blower Junior was commissioned by American actor Jason Momoa.

title: The Polestar 2 Is Returning To North America
summary: The fan favorite EV is going back on sale in North America, thanks to Canada’s openness to Chinese-built electric cars.

title: The Volvo XC60 Plug-In Hybrid Will Get A Serious Range Boost: Report
summary: The current XC60 T8 plug-in hybrid can go 35 miles on electric power. The 2028 model will reportedly nearly triple that.

title: It's Still Way More Expensive To Insure An EV. But That's Changing
summary: Newer EVs cost about 18% more to insure than gas vehicles. Here's why.

title: The Rolls-Royce Spectre Gets Big Update. But You Wouldn’t Know It
summary: It now goes farther, charges faster, and responds more sharply, but the design remains mostly untouched.
